revision.text:
[[ja:天健金草神社]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Amatakekanakaya Shrine | native_name = 天健金草神社 | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Amatakekanakaya Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|36.205681|133.230887}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Amatakekanakaya Shrine'''|天健金草神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Oki Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|lt=Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * {{ill|Shikinai Hiteisha|lt=Shikinai Hiteisha|ja|比定社|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11659043|ltq=Q11659043|qid=Q11659043|1=List of Shikinaisha in Oki Province}} ** Series ordinal: 13 ** P155: {{ill|Wakenosu Shrine|lt=Wakenosu Shrine|ja|和気能須神社|qid=Q135041143|1=Wakenosu Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q11549109|ltq=Q11549109|qid=Q11549109|1=Mizuwakasu Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Oki Province|lt=Oki Province|cs|Provincie Oki|de|Provinz Oki|en|Oki Province|es|Provincia de Oki|fa|ولایت اوکی|fr|Province d'Oki|id|Provinsi Oki|it|Oki (provincia)|ja|隠岐国|ko|오키국|lt|Okio žemė|nl|Oki (provincie)|pt|Província de Oki|ru|Оки (провинция)|simple|Oki Province|sk|Oki (provincia)|sr|Провинција Оки|tr|Oki (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Окі|zh_min_nan|Oki-kok|zh|隱岐國|qid=Q1063829|1=Oki Province}} * {{ill|Q1205039|ltq=Q1205039|qid=Q1205039|1=Okinoshima, Shimane}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|36.205681|133.230887}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 183004 ** P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Q11071125|ltq=Q11071125|qid=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth Rank}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|ko|종4위|zh|從四位|qid=Q11071127|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} * {{ill|Q11123338|ltq=Q11123338|qid=Q11123338|1=Senior Fourth Rank}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県隠岐郡隠岐の島町都万', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:天健金草神社]] {{wikidata link|Q135041144}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Amatake Kanakaya Shrine |Image = [[File:Amatakekanakaya-Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |Location = {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|qid=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Oki District|Q735735|qid=Q735735|1=Oki District, Shimane}}{{ill|lt=Okinoshima Town|Q1205039|qid=Q1205039|1=Okinoshima, Shimane}} 4243 Reikizan, Sunasakodani, Tsuma {{sfn|Shimane Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines}} |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōyatsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsumatsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997}}{{sfn|Handa|1983|p=984}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (minor shrine)|Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}{{sfn|Handa|1983|p=985}} |Founded = |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}{{sfn|Handa|1983|p=986}} |Alternate names = |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = April 15{{ref|Shimane Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Amatake
Kanakaya Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} '''Amatake Kanakaya Shrine''' (あまたけかなかやじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Tsuma, {{ill|lt=Okinoshima Town|Q1205039|qid=Q1205039|1=Okinoshima, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Oki District|Q735735|qid=Q735735|1=Oki District, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|qid=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor shrine), and its former shrine rank was Prefectural Shrine. === History === It is said that the enshrined deities of this shrine displayed divine efficacy during {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|qid=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}’s {{ill|lt=conquest of the Three Kingdoms of Korea|Q11249435|qid=Q11249435|1=Three Han Campaign}}, and that offerings were presented in the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|qid=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}} (193) {{sfn|Tsuma Village History Editorial Committee|1990|p=88}}. According to the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|qid=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, in the 13th year of {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} (871), the shrine was granted the divine rank of Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade, in recognition of its contribution to prayers for national defense against {{ill|lt=Silla|Q28456|qid=Q28456|1=Silla}} {{sfn|Tsuma Village History Editorial Committee|1990|p=86}}. According to the {{ill|lt=Nihon Kiryaku|Q1053542|qid=Q1053542|1=Nihon Kiryaku}}, in the 6th year of {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|qid=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} (906), Amatake Kanakaya-no-Mikoto caused “several Silla pirate ships” to retreat by means of a great wind {{sfn|Tsuma Village History Editorial Committee|1990|pp=86–87}}, and in the 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|qid=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} (940) was granted Junior Third Rank {{sfn|Handa|1983|p=985}}. With the exception of {{ill|lt=Kumano Taisha|Q11568887|qid=Q11568887|1=Kumano Taisha}} and {{ill|lt=Izumo Taisha|Q696362|qid=Q696362|1=Izumo-taisha}}, this divine rank was outstanding in the San’in region {{sfn|Handa|1983|p=985}}. In the 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Kankō|Q2736722|qid=Q2736722|1=Kankō}} (1006), the shrine was relocated to its present site {{sfn|Tsuma Village History Editorial Committee|1990|p=1215}}. In the medieval period, {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|Q313942|qid=Q313942|1=Emperor Go-Daigo}}, who had been exiled to Oki, prayed at this shrine for his return to the capital, and when he succeeded in escaping from Oki, he granted it the rank of Senior First Rank {{sfn|Tsuma Village History Editorial Committee|1990|p=89}}{{efn|There is also an opinion that this Senior First Rank corresponds to a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sōgen senji|1=UNKNOWN}}{{sfn|Handa|1983|p=985}}.}}. However, during this period, the Hachiman Shrine that was jointly enshrined became the primary focus of worship, and furthermore, {{ill|lt=Takada Shrine (Okinoshima Town)|UNKNOWN|lt=Takada Shrine}} came to attract the devotion of many people in this area {{sfn|Tsuma Village History Editorial Committee|1990|p=1164}}. In the first year of {{ill|lt=Bunkyū|Q730266|qid=Q730266|1=Bunkyū}} (1861), a fire destroyed not only the shrine buildings but also many treasures and ancient records {{sfn|Tsuma Village History Editorial Committee|1990|p=1216}}. In Meiji 5 (1872), the shrine was designated a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=modern shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, and later, in 1934 (Shōwa 9), it was elevated to Prefectural Shrine {{sfn|Tsuma Village History Editorial Committee|1990|p=1216}}. === Enshrined deities === === Principal deities === * '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōyatsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' (おおやつひめのみこと) * '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsumatsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' (つまつひめのみこと) : These two deities are children of {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} and are said to have devoted themselves to land development, after which they resided respectively at “Ōya Sentō” and “Sayama Spring” {{sfn|Tsuma Village History Editorial Committee|1990|p=88}}. These two locations lie several kilometers from this shrine and were regarded as sacred sites where the deities descended, but eventually this shrine was founded at a place called “Hachimanbara,” where they came to be enshrined {{sfn|Tsuma Village History Editorial Committee|1990|p=88}}. There is a tradition that derives the place name “Tsuma” from Tsumatsuhime-no-Mikoto, but it is also said that the deity came to be enshrined based on an association with the place name {{sfn|Rika no Sha|1987|p=200}}. * '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997}}''' (ほんだわけのみこと) : One of the deities of the jointly enshrined Hachiman Shrine {{sfn|Handa|1983|p=984}}. === Subsidiary deities === * '''Okinagatara-shi-hime-no-Mikoto''' (おきながたらしひめのみこと) : One of the deities of the jointly enshrined Hachiman Shrine {{sfn|Handa|1983|p=984}}. * '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tamayori-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' (たまよりひめのみこと) : One of the deities of the jointly enshrined Hachiman Shrine {{sfn|Handa|1983|p=984}}. * '''{{ill|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji|Q11174872|qid=Q11174872|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji|1=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji}}''' (しおつちのおじ) {{sfn|Handa|1983|p=984}} : One of the deities of the jointly enshrined Hachiman Shrine {{sfn|Handa|1983|p=984}}. * '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata|qid=Q9150391}}''' (たけみなかたのみこと) : The deity of Suwa Shrine that was jointly enshrined in 1917 {{sfn|Handa|1983|p=984}}. Although this contradicts historical fact, there is a tradition that this Suwa Shrine was established in the first year of {{ill|lt=Shōji|Q1199890|qid=Q1199890|1=Shōji (era)}} (1199) by {{ill|lt=Sasaki Yasukiyo|Q11382304|qid=Q11382304|1=Sasaki Yasukiyo}} through a transfer from {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|qid=Q218813|1=Suwa-taisha}} {{sfn|Tsuma Village History Editorial Committee|1990|p=116}}. === Facilities === [[File:Amatakekanakaya-Jinja Front.jpg|thumb|Shrine grounds of Amatake Kanakaya Shrine]] [[File:Amatakekanakaya-Jinja Shrine.jpg|thumb|Main hall of Amatake Kanakaya Shrine]] ; Main hall : A three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure with copper-sheet roofing {{sfn|Takahashi|1980|p=22}}{{efn|There is also a description stating iron-sheet roofing {{sfn|Handa|1983|p=986}}.}}, with a size of 7 tsubo {{sfn|Handa|1983|p=986}}. The framework was erected in the first year of {{ill|lt=Genji|Q1325419|qid=Q1325419|1=Genji era}} (1864) {{sfn|Handa|1983|p=986}}. It has been noted that its floor plan resembles {{ill|lt=Oki-zukuri|Q11659055|qid=Q11659055|1=Oki-zukuri}} {{sfn|Takahashi|1980|p=23}}. ; Worship hall : An {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|qid=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} structure with black tile roofing, measuring 11.5 tsubo {{sfn|Handa|1983|p=986}}. Built in 1917 (Taishō 6) {{sfn|Handa|1983|p=986}}. ; Offering hall : An irimoya-zukuri structure with black tile roofing, measuring 6 tsubo {{sfn|Handa|1983|p=986}}. ; Zuijin Gate : A {{ill|lt=kirizuma-zukuri|Q13308575|qid=Q13308575|1=Kirizuma-style}} structure with board roofing {{sfn|Handa|1983|p=986}}. ; Shrine office : An irimoya-zukuri structure with black tile roofing {{sfn|Handa|1983|p=986}}. ; Auxiliary shrines : Takeuchi Shrine (enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668|qid=Q1073668|lt=Takeuchi no Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}) {{sfn|Tsuma Village History Editorial Committee|1990|p=1217}} : Kasuga Shrine (enshrined deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qid=Q2564508}}) {{sfn|Tsuma Village History Editorial Committee|1990|p=1217}} : Manito Shrine (enshrined deities: fifteen goddesses) {{sfn|Tsuma Village History Editorial Committee|1990|p=1217}} ; Other : One Myōjin torii made of {{ill|lt=Shii|Q1065400|qid=Q1065400|lt=chinquapin|1=Castanopsis}} wood {{sfn|Handa|1983|p=986}}. One pair each of stone lanterns and karajishi lion-dogs {{sfn|Handa|1983|p=986}}. : The former shrine site is located at Hachimanbara, Tsuma, Okinoshima Town, and foundation stones remain {{sfn|Handa|1983|p=986}}. === Footnotes === === Notes === {{notelist}} === References === {{reflist|20em}} === Bibliography === * {{cite book|Japanese|author=Yasuo Takahashi|chapter=Second Survey|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/12422280/1/7|editor=Cultural Properties Management Section, Cultural Affairs Division|title=Emergency Survey Report on Early Modern Shrine and Temple Architecture in Shimane Prefecture|publisher=Shimane Prefectural Board of Education|year=1980|pages=5–54|id={{JP number|81001279}}|ref={{sfnref|Takahashi|1980}}}} * {{cite book|Japanese|editor=Tsuma Village History Editorial Committee|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/13227577|title=History of Tsuma Village|year=1990|id={{JP number|91014641}}|ref={{sfnref|Tsuma Village History Editorial Committee|1990}}}} * {{Cite book |Japanese |author=Yaichirō Handa |chapter=13 Amatake Kanakaya Shrine |editor=Shikinaisha Research Association |title=Shikinaisha Survey Report, Vol. 21, San’indō 4 |publisher=Kōgakkan University Press |year=1983 |pages=981–986 |id={{JP number|83032918}} |ref={{sfnref|Handa|1983}} }} * {{Cite book |Japanese |author=Rika no Sha |chapter=489. Tsumatsuhime-no-Mikoto |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/13275772/1/107 |title=Gods of Shimane |publisher=Shimane Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines |id={{JP number|88012198}} |year=1987 |pages=200–201 |ref={{sfnref|Rika no Sha|1987}} }} * {{cite web|title=Amatake Kanakaya Shrine – Shrine Details|url=https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/okinoshima/bd697e5a9907b008a6344ffd2286735ea0d99133.html|publisher=Shimane Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|accessdate=2025-10-01|ref={{sfnref|Shimane Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines}}}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Oki Province|Q11659043|qid=Q11659043|1=List of Shikinaisha in Oki Province}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Amatakekanakaya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Amatakekanakaya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Amatakekanakaya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Oki Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Oki Province]] [[Category:Municipal History of Okinoshima]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages with 50+ untranslated japanese characters]]
